item.title: Selection for the admission to the Theory and Numerical Simulation of the Condensed Matter PhD course - Fellowsiph financed by Chiesi Farmaceutici Project: Simulating therapeutic aerosol deposition in human and animal lung models
item.description:
Responsabili del Corso / Heads of PhD course: Andrea BENASSI Georgi Hristov SPASOV

From: 16-01-2026 13:00
To: 26-02-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Duration: 4 years


Selection Process:  Academic qualifications - interview (remotely) (16 - 20/03/2026)


Simulating therapeutic aerosol deposition in human and animal lung models


 


Delivering medications in and through the lungs of patients, in the form of inhaled aerosols, is a particularly challenging task as 260K years of natural selection made the lungs a very good filter for particles and droplets spanning several orders of magnitude in size [1]. The complexity comes from many different factors: the self-affine (fractal) structure of the lung airways; the multi-phase nature of the air flow transporting solid particle or liquid droplets; the highly non-linear behaviour of the multi-phase flow; the multi scale nature of turbulence developing in the extra-thoracic airways (mouth/nose, throat and trachea); the size of the aerosol particles spanning a large range, from mm to nm; the physical and chemical transformation taking place in and on the transported aerosol particles as they travel into the patient lungs.


Mathematical modelling and numerical simulations proved to be good tools to help the design of novel drug products for inhalation. Their development poses interesting challenging to researchers interested in classical and statistical mechanics, bio-engineering and computational sciences [2]. Besides understanding and optimizing the design of new drug products, the possibility to create virtual patient lungs, aka lung digital twins, offers the interesting long-term perspective to replace long and expensive clinical trials with their in-silico counterpart. The same is true for toxicology and pharmacokinetic studies, if lung simulations could be extended to animal models (e.g. rodents of various kind such as mice, rats and guinea pigs) [3]. Through the PhD fellowship the candidate will contribute to: develop computational models of human and animal lungs starting from medical imaging data acquired on real patients and animals; apply realistic and physiological mechanical ventilation models to the generated virtual lungs; develop mathematical models of different lung pathologies to be applied to the generated virtual lungs; study therapeutical aerosol deposition in digital lung models through Monte Carlo deposition models and other stochastic/statistical methods [4].


The ideal PhD candidate should have a master degree in mathematics, physics, fluid mechanics engineering, bio-engineering or medical engineering. Given the computational nature of the thesis programming skills and knowledge of C/C++ and Python are very welcome. Knowledge of parallel computing and some prior experience in running scientific computing in large high performance computing facilities are also a plus. A significant part of the thesis activities will be conducted in collaboration with Chiesi Farmaceutici, a pharmaceutical company with main R&D labs based in Parma, (Italy) [5]. The candidate will work in an international and muti-disciplinary environment with researchers in physics, engineering, medicine and pharmaceutical technology. A fluent English is necessary as well as the propension for team-working and communication.


Interested candidates can send their CV or ask for more information through the email address a.benassiatchiesi.com.


 


[1] Breath Taking, M.J.Stephen, Grove Press (2021)


[2] The mechanics of inhaled pharmaceutical aerosols. W.H. Finlay, Academic Press (2001)


[3] G.H. Spasov et al. Modeling and simulation of the human respiratory system Part 1: Computational fluid dynamics simulations and whole lung models, Inhalation Magazine (December 2024 issue)


G.H. Spasov et al. Modeling and simulation of the human respiratory system Part 2: Inhaler design, in vitro experiments and imaging, Inhalation Magazine (April 2025 issue)


[4] Spasov, G.H.; Cottini, C.; Benassi, A. A Monte Carlo-Based 3D Whole Lung Model for Aerosol Deposition Studies: Implementation and Validation, Bioengineering (2025), 12, 1092


[5] https://www.chiesi.com/en/home/

item.title: Bando di ammissione al corso di PhD in Astrofisica e Cosmologia / Selection for the admission to the Astrophysics and Cosmology PhD course
item.description:
Responsabile del Corso / Head of PhD course: Prof. Carlo BACCIGALUPI

From: 18-12-2025 13:00
To: 13-03-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Bando di ammissione al corso di PhD in Astrofisica e Cosmologia / Selection for the admission to the Astrophysics and Cosmology PhD course


Topics:


• Analysis of Astrophysical and Cosmological Datasets

• Astrochemistry

• Dark Matter and Dark Energy

• Galaxy Formation and Evolution

• Gravitational Waves

• High Energy Astrophysics

• Physical Cosmology

• Stellar Physics


Durata / Duration: 4 anni / 4 years


Modalità di selezione  / Selection Process:  Titoli  - prova orale / Academic qualifications - interview (31.03 - 01-04.2026) - Candidates can choose whether they wish to attend in person or remotely

item.title: Selection for the admission to the Molecular and Statistical Biophysics PhD course - Decoding and designing biomolecular systems with hybrid DNA:RNA: protein nanotechnology (BIOHYBRITE)
item.description:
Responsabile del Corso / Head of PhD course: Prof. Cristian MICHELETTI

From: 30-12-2025 13:00
To: 11-05-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Position DC12 - Predictive modelling of translocation properties of nucleic acids and protein assemblies


Primary academic institution: Prof. Cristian Micheletti (SISSA)


Associated advisory team: Prof. Ulrich Keyser, UCAM


Project Description

This PhD project has a planned duration of 48 months, including the 36 months funded under the BioHYBRITE (MSCA Doctoral Networks) action plus an additional 12 months of SISSA fellowship. The project will develop and apply advanced theoretical and computational models for two purposes. First, it will characterize how the structure of nucleic acids, proteins, and their biohybrid assemblies affects their motion through nanopores. The results will then be used to design molecules with desired pore translocation properties.


The theoretical/computational project will involve close international collaborations (planned secondments) with experimental and theoretical partner groups of the BioHybrite network, through which the doctoral researcher will iteratively refine models by comparing them with experiments and, finally, formulate predictions.


Modalità di selezione: Titoli  - scritto - orale (da remoto)

Selection Process:  Academic qualifications - written - interview (remotely)

25/05 - 26 & 27/05/2026

To: 20-03-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Bando di ammissione al corso di PhD in Neuroscienze Cognitive

Selection for the admission to the Cognitive Neuroscience PhD course


Topics:


•             Main topics in cognitive neuroscience

•             Neuronal bases of Visual perception

•             Neuronal bases of Time perception

•             SISSA Explorations into the Neuronal foundations of Sensory Experience (SENSEx)

•             Neuronal bases of multimodal communication

•             Neural computation approaches to memory and brain organization

•             Computational bases of perception and cognition


Durata / Duration: 4 anni / 4 years


Modalità di selezione  / Selection Process:  Titoli  - orale (da remoto)

Academic qualifications - interview (remotely)

13/04 - 14/04/2026

• Prion biology

• Computational genomics

item.title: Bando di ammissione al corso di PhD in Biofisica Statistica e Molecolare / Selection for the admission to the Molecular and Statistical Biophysics PhD course
item.description:
Responsabile del Corso / Head of PhD course: Prof. Giovanni BUSSI

From: 18-12-2025 13:00
To: 11-05-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Avviso di selezione ad evidenza pubblica per l'ammissione ai Corsi di Dottorato di ricerca  - phd-msb-01-2026


Topics:


• Statistical mechanics of complex molecular systems

• Activity-driven biological processes

• Stochastic processes and biological noise

• Biomolecular simulations

• Simulations of rare events

• Soft Matter Physics


Durata / Duration: 4 anni / 4 years


Modalità di selezione  / Selection Process:  Titoli  - scritto - orale (da remoto) Academic qualifications - written - interview (remotely) (25/05 - 26 & 27/05/2026)

item.title: Bando di ammissione al corso di PhD in Neurobiologia / Selection for the admission to the Neurobiology PhD course
item.description:
Responsabile del Corso / Head of PhD course: Prof. Laura BALLERINI

From: 18-12-2025 13:00
To: 25-03-2026 13:00
Category: Dottorati

Avviso di selezione ad evidenza pubblica per l'ammissione ai Corsi di Dottorato di ricerca - phd-neurobio-01-2026


Topics:


• Peripheral Nervous System

• Pain, touch and itch

• Synaptic Neurophysiology and Neuronal Networks

• Optogenetic and electrophysiological approaches to Neuronal Networks

• Neurotoxicity and calcium signalling

• Spatiotemporal dynamics of calcium signalling in neuroglia

• Neurophysiology of Motor control

• Spinal networks patho-physiology and locomotion

• Respiratory physiology

• Neurophysiology and neuropharmacology of spinal motoneurons

• Visual systems and feature detection

• Simultaneous acquisition of neural activity and behavior

• Innate behaviors and their underlying circuits

• Functional imaging approaches to neural circuits

• Neuroecology

• Auditory system and multisensory integration

• Computational neuroscience and modelling

• Brain states and neuromodulation

• Sensory information processing abnormalities in psychiatric disorders


Durata / Duration: 4 anni / 4 years


Modalità di selezione  / Selection Process:  Titoli  - orale (da remoto) Academic qualifications -  interview (remotely) ( 22.04 - 23.04.2026)
